This framework translates user-input natural language intents into network configurations and continuously updates these configurations based on computations of downstream network states to achieve on-demand resource allocation. To accomplish this, we designed two main modules: the Intent Translator and the Resource Allocator. In the Intent Translator, we propose an LLM-empowered intent translation approach. The introduction of RAG enhances the performance of LLMs in intent translation tasks, effectively mitigating hallucinations and generating higher-quality, deployable commands. In the Resource Allocator, we present an LLM-guided resource allocation method. Leveraging their strong contextual understanding, LLMs can better analyze the attributes of emerging services, thereby guiding the training and inference of resource allocation models. Through this approach, the proposed framework achieves high-quality intent translation and efficient resource allocation, enabling intent-driven network autonomy.
authors: L. Guo; L. Zhang; J. Wang; J. Wu; Y. Yan; H. Sun; B. He; Q. Qi; J. Liao
source: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
keywords: Autonomous network;intent translation;resource allocation;large language model

title: Zero-Shot End-to-End Relation Extraction in Chinese: A Comparative Study of Gemini, LLaMA, and ChatGPT
abstract: This study investigates the performance of various large language models (LLMs) on zero-shot end-to-end relation extraction (RE) in Chinese, a task that integrates entity recognition and relation extraction without requiring annotated data. While LLMs show promise for RE, most prior work focuses on English or assumes pre-annotated entities, leaving their effectiveness in Chinese RE largely unexplored. To bridge this gap, we evaluate ChatGPT, Gemini, and LLaMA based on accuracy, efficiency, and adaptability. ChatGPT demonstrates the highest overall performance, balancing precision and recall, while Gemini achieves the fastest inference speed, making it suitable for real-time applications. LLaMA underperforms in both accuracy and latency, highlighting the need for further adaptation. Our findings provide insights into the strengths and limitations of LLMs for zero-shot Chinese RE, shedding light on trade-offs between accuracy and efficiency. This study serves as a foundation for future research aimed at improving LLM adaptability to complex linguistic tasks in Chinese NLP.
authors: S. Du; Y. Tao; Y. Shen; H. Zhang; Y. Shen; X. Qiu; C. Shi
source: 2025 8th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
keywords: zero-shot learning;end-to-end relation extraction;large language models;Chinese NLP;ChatGPT;Gemini;LLaMA;performance evaluation

title: Construction and Application of Traditional Chinese Medicine English Knowledge Graph Driven by Intelligent Algorithms
abstract: Fragmented Traditional Chinese Medicine English knowledge hinders global dissemination. To address this, this study proposes an intelligent algorithm-driven framework to construct a comprehensive TCM English knowledge graph. The method integrates NLP and machine learning: Bi-LSTM+CRF for entity recognition, CNN for relation extraction, and a rule-neural two-stage optimization that reduces invalid relations by 15%. Structurally, the graph is organized around a 5-layer TCM-theory-based ontology, integrating multilingual terminologies and hierarchical relationships. An application system enables intelligent querying, context-aware retrieval, and both rule-based and machine learning-based reasoning. Experiments on a large dataset show a 30% higher accuracy in semantic matching and knowledge inference than keyword-based systems, with 88.6% semantic retrieval accuracy and 91.3% disambiguation for key terms. Results demonstrate that the approach enhances TCM knowledge access precision and efficiency while resolving crosslingual challenges. By establishing a computable knowledge infrastructure, it provides robust technical support for TCM internationalization and more accessible global medical communication.
authors: Y. Yuan; X. Lang
source: 2025 2nd International Conference on Software, Systems and Information Technology (SSITCON)
keywords: traditional chinese medicine;knowledge graph;intelligent algorithms;machine learning;global medical communication

title: Leveraging Multilingual Transfer for Unsupervised Semantic Acoustic Word Embeddings
abstract: Acoustic word embeddings (AWEs) are fixed-dimensional vector representations of speech segments that encode phonetic content so that different realisations of the same word have similar embeddings. In this letter we explore semantic AWE modelling. These AWEs should not only capture phonetics but also the meaning of a word (similar to textual word embeddings). We consider the scenario where we only have untranscribed speech in a target language. We introduce a number of strategies leveraging a pre-trained multilingual AWE model: a phonetic AWE model trained on labelled data from multiple languages excluding the target. Our best semantic AWE approach involves clustering word segments using the multilingual AWE model, deriving soft pseudo-word labels from the cluster centroids, and then training a Skipgram-like model on the soft vectors. In an intrinsic word similarity task measuring semantics, this multilingual transfer approach outperforms all previous semantic AWE methods. We also show—for the first time—that AWEs can be used for downstream semantic query-by-example search.
authors: C. Jacobs; H. Kamper
source: IEEE Signal Processing Letters
keywords: Acoustic word embeddings;query-by-example search;semantic embeddings;semantic retrieval

title: An Evaluation of LLMs and Google Translate for Translation of Selected Indian Languages via Sentiment and Semantic Analyses
abstract: Large Language models (LLMs) have been prominent for language translation, including low-resource languages. There has been limited study on the assessment of the quality of translations generated by LLMs, including Gemini, GPT, and Google Translate. This study addresses this limitation by using semantic and sentiment analysis of selected LLMs for Indian languages, including Sanskrit, Telugu and Hindi. We select prominent texts (Bhagavad Gita, Tamas and Maha Prasthanam) that have been well translated by experts and use LLMs to generate their translations into English, and provide a comparison with selected expert (human) translations. Our investigation revealed that while LLMs have made significant progress in translation accuracy, challenges remain in preserving sentiment and semantic integrity, especially in metaphorical and philosophical contexts for texts such as the Bhagavad Gita. The sentiment analysis revealed that GPT models are better at preserving the sentiment polarity for the given texts when compared to human (expert) translation. The results revealed that GPT models are generally better at maintaining the sentiment and semantics when compared to Google Translate. This study could help in the development of accurate and culturally sensitive translation systems for large language models.
authors: R. Chandra; A. Chaudhari; Y. Rayavarapu
source: IEEE Access
keywords: LLMs;sentiment analysis;machine translation;semantic analysis

title: An Intelligent Terminology Recommendation System for Real-Time Translation of Technical Documents
abstract: In multilingual translation of technical documents, terminological consistency significantly affects both the professionalism and comprehensibility of the target text. To address the insufficient terminology support in existing translation systems, this paper proposes and implements an intelligent terminology recommendation system tailored for real-time technical document translation. The system integrates modules for terminology recognition, contextual matching, candidate recommendation, and translation injection. Leveraging deep learning architectures and semantic vector techniques, the system provides real-time suggestions. Experimental results demonstrate that the system significantly enhances the accuracy and consistency of terminology translation, offering reliable support for human–machine collaborative translation. This system represents a step forward in AI-powered translation support tools.
authors: W. Sun; W. Wang
source: 2025 2nd International Conference on Software, Systems and Information Technology (SSITCON)
keywords: terminology translation;real-time recommendation;technical documents;natural language processing;computer-assisted translation

title: Automatic Privacy Policy Compliance Assessment Leveraging NLP Models
abstract: Following its adoption in 2018, the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) has impacted data processing entities significantly, especially by compelling them to ensure their privacy policies are compliant with a defined set of requirements. Manual assessment of GDPR compliance is laborious and susceptible to errors, driving increased research interest in automated solutions with such capabilities. This paper provides a systematic review of the recent advancements in the automated evaluation of privacy policies employing Natural Language Processing (NLP) methods. Within this context, this study proposes a structured approach for processing Romanian privacy policies, a particularly complex task, considering both the low-resource status of the Romanian language and the intricacy of the regulatory wording utilized. Aiming to address these constraints, this paper proposes a compliance verification system, leveraging the semantic similarity capabilities of the E5 Transformer model. Experimental results indicated that the trained model achieved a 75% accuracy in matching sentences extracted from a Romanian privacy policy to the appropriate GDPR requirements. Furthermore, the paper tackles the challenges of adapting NLP-based solutions to other languages with scarce resources, providing insights and directions for future research in multilingual and domain-specific settings.
authors: C. -M. Priescu; M. -A. Moisescu; M. -E. Iliuță
source: 2025 25th International Conference on Control Systems and Computer Science (CSCS)
keywords: GDPR Compliance;Privacy Policies;NLP;Semantic Similarity Analysis;Low-resource Languages;E5 Transformer Model

title: Next generation Legal technology for Leveraging Deep Learning and LLMs for Legal Judgment Prediction
abstract: Legal professionals and judicial bodies often face the challenge of analyzing extensive legal documents, identifying applicable constitutional articles, and referring to relevant precedent. It is a time-consuming and inconsistent process. The core challenge of digital case records lies in automating legal analysis while maintaining accuracy, interpretability, and fairness. Traditional systems rely on rule-based or keyword-matching methods fail to understand the semantic depth of legal language. Additionally, retrieving precedent cases and aligning them with constitutional provisions demands a contextual understanding that static systems lack. Hence, this paper introduces an AI-powered legal analysis system that leverages deep learning and Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) to provide real-time legal judgment prediction and relevant case retrieval. The system employs Google Pegasus for abstractive legal text summarization, efficiently condensing complex case documents while preserving critical information and intent. The system architecture includes - Legal Text Summarization using Google Pegasus to generate concise representations of case details, Article Matching and Retrieval via RAG to identify applicable constitutional provisions, and Relevant Case Retrieval leveraging semantic embeddings to fetch past cases with contextual similarity. The key improvements include a significant reduction in manual workload, enhanced consistency in legal interpretations, and real-time generation of judgment reports. The integration of summarization, semantic matching, and intelligent retrieval ensures both accuracy and scalability. Hence, the system delivers a smart, scalable solution for automating legal analysis. It enhances judicial efficiency, supports fair decision-making, and empowers legal professionals with accurate AI-generated insights and paving the way for next-generation legal technology.
authors: A. K. R; G. Likith Sai; A. D; S. Sivaranjani; K. Dev R
source: 2025 International Conference on Next Generation Computing Systems (ICNGCS)
keywords: Large Language Models;Domain-Specific AI;Retrieval-Augmented Generation;Nomic-Em- beddings;Legal Technology;Legal Assistance

title: SMART-Eq: Structured and unstructured Market Analysis using Retrieval Techniques and LLMs for Equities
abstract: Forecasting equity markets has traditionally relied on structured data sources, such as historical prices, fundamentals, and macroeconomic indicators. However, unstructured data, including analyst reports, earnings-call transcripts, and financial news, can provide valuable qualitative insights to enhance these forecasts. The proposed system employs a multi-stage pipeline that includes data ingestion, domain-specific text chunking, and dual-indexing (lexical and vector-based) to support hybrid retrieval. Four retrieval paradigms are evaluated—traditional text search, vector-based retrieval, hybrid retrieval, and vector-based retrieval with re-ranking—on a custom dataset of 315 financial documents, augmented by 20 control questions, and validated against the FinQA dataset. Experimental results, assessed via ROUGE metrics and expert evaluations, indicate that incorporating vectorization and re-ranking not only improves the precision of retrieved information but also enhances the contextual accuracy of LLM-generated responses. The findings demonstrate that SMART-Eq effectively bridges qualitative insights with quantitative data, offering a robust and scalable approach for enhanced equity market analysis and forecasting. The paper concludes with a discussion of integration challenges and future research directions, including domain-adapted LLM fine-tuning and real-time data processing.
authors: A. S. Chandra; A. S. Chandra; V. Gahlot
source: 2025 World Skills Conference on Universal Data Analytics and Sciences (WorldSUAS)
keywords: hybrid data;structured data;unstructured data;equity markets;financial forecasting;equity forecasting;large language models (LLMs);natural language processing (NLP);semantic retrieval;vector embeddings;hybrid retrieval;generativeAI;SMART-Eq

title: NLP-Powered Resume Screening and Ranking System
abstract: Indeed, the system employs Natural Language Processing (NLP) techniques to identify the main features in the resume, including skills, education, and work experience. It then compares these features with the job description by means of semantic similarity methods, such as TF-IDF (Term Frequency Inverse Document Frequency) and BERT (Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers) embeddings. The ranking engine uses weighted scoring algorithms that prioritize the relevancy of resumes. Further, the system also needs the user to upload their resume and job description through the web-based interface where the output will display that a listing has been generated and is listed to be gone through with the candidates. They evaluate the performance of the system by means of evaluation metrics such as precision, recall, F1-score, and Mean Reciprocal Rank (MRR). In their results, it shows that BERT based semantic matching has proved better than the conventional keyword-based methods as far as accuracy and efficiency in screening resumes is concerned. What makes the current study noteworthy is that artificial intelligence (AI) could help improve recruiting automation and point to ways of streamlining the hiring process. This will bring significant reductions to bias and further improve the fairness and effectiveness of selecting a candidate. Further work contemplates multi-lingual support, domain-specific customizations, and integration of the system to Applicant Tracking Systems (ATS) to increase the importance of this study in real-world terms.
authors: T. A. Dharmendra; K. Meenakshi; D. Bhargava
source: 2025 3rd International Conference on Disruptive Technologies (ICDT)
keywords: AI in Recruitment;Applicant Tracking System (ATS);Automated Resume Screening;HR Technology;Job Matching;NLP (Natural Language Processing);Recruitment Automation;Resume Ranking

title: Construction of a Recurrent Neural Network Machine Translation Framework Based on Data Enhancement and Attention Mechanisms
abstract: In order to improve the semantic modeling ability and translation quality of recurrent neural networks in machine translation tasks, a neural translation framework that incorporates data enhancement and multi-level attention mechanisms is constructed. The research design includes text-level semantic perturbation, language model-based context generation strategy and gated recalibration with multi-scale context fusion in the attention enhancement module, which systematically improves the model’s generalization performance and semantic alignment accuracy in low-resource environments. Comparison experiments on the WMT14 English-German dataset show that the constructed model outperforms the baseline model in BLEU, perplexity and syntactic coverage, especially in long sentence translation and complex syntactic structure processing, which verifies the effectiveness of the structural optimization and strategy integration.
authors: J. Huang
source: 2025 5th International Symposium on Computer Technology and Information Science (ISCTIS)
keywords: recurrent neural networks;data augmentation;attention mechanism;neural machine translation

title: Reinforced Multilingual Geospatial Query Extraction Using Naive Bayes and Fuzzy Matching
abstract: The paper introduces a method for automatic canonicalization of geospatial entities from an unstructured language input given by the user. The traditional and old method of Geospatial canonical search is outdated and has loopholes for the handling of the multilingual input and most importantly the accuracy of the model. The system is designed to handle Textual input and Voice Modulated input. To detect and correct the non- standardized geospatial entities, which includes those with typographical errors or unconventional spellings, we use the advanced natural language processing (NLP) technique for standardizing the entities. By using the custom Naive Bayes probability algorithm, the Model identifies potential geospatial entities by recognizing the incorrectly spelled names and matching them with their correct form using the naive bayes probability algorithm. Furthermore, to enhance the identification of entities accuracy we use the system which has some per-defined list of abbreviations for geographical features like (mountains, lakes, oceans etc.) In case the where the same place name exists in multiple locations in same or different country (like Punjab in both India and Pakistan), then the Model provides the geographical output data for all the relevant locations with their exact longitude and latitude coordinates. After this the model triggers the fuzzy matching algorithm which uses the token ratio technique to ensure the precise mapping of corrected names to their canonical forms in the dataset.
authors: A. Ghosh; M. Singh; A. Mishra; A. K. Prasad; S. Kaur
source: 2024 IEEE 1st International Conference on Green Industrial Electronics and Sustainable Technologies (GIEST)
keywords: Geospatial Query System;Fuzzy Matching Algorithm;Web application;canonical names;Named Entity Recognition (NER) etc

title: An Inner-Attention Based LSTM Network for Semantic Matching and Recommendation
abstract: The paper proposes a joint neural network model to overcome the problems of semantic loss and information sparsity in short texts, allowing the task processing system to focus on finding significantly relevant information in the input data for the current output. Our method applies the attention mechanism in Semantic Matching and Recommendation that tries to simulate human attentive behavior for query and retrieval. Experimental results indicate the method proves to be more effective than the existing sentence encoding-based approaches in terms of Content-based recommendation.
authors: J. Li; Y. Chen
source: 2024 8th Asian Conference on Artificial Intelligence Technology (ACAIT)
keywords: LSTM;content-based recommendation;inner-attention mechanism;semantic matching

title: Deep Learning-based Semantic Search Techniques for Enhancing Product Matching in E-commerce
abstract: Searching is the process of information retrieval utilizing specific criteria or keywords. Integrating search function-alities on e-commerce platforms enables users to efficiently locate exactly what they are searching for through keyword matching. Beyond conventional keyword matching, semantic search involves aligning products with customer queries by capturing the essence of the queries, thereby retrieving semantically related products from the pertinent catalog. Semantic search enhances the e-commerce shopping experience by allowing platforms to tailor responses to user preferences through an in-depth understanding of search intents. Challenges such as morphological variations, spelling errors, and the interpretation of synonyms, antonyms, and hypernyms are addressed through deep learning models de-signed for semantic query-product matching. This study conducts a comparative analysis of various semantic search methodologies and assesses their efficacy, incorporating deep learning strate-gies for query auto-completion and spelling corrections. The evaluation employs sentence transformer models to determine the optimal approach for semantic searching, gauged by nDCG, MRR, and MAP metrics. LSTM, BART, and n-gram models are also examined for auto-completion capabilities. The research analyzes the Amazon Shopping Queries Dataset and the Upstart Commerce catalog datasets.
authors: F. Aamir; R. Sherafgan; T. Arbab; A. Jamil; F. N. Bhatti; A. A. Hameed
source: 2024 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Computing and Machine Intelligence (ICMI)
keywords: Semantic product searching;Natural Language Processing (NLP);Machine Learning;E-commerce

title: Domain-Enhanced SimCSE with Lexicon Injection for Semantic Representation of Technical Texts
abstract: This study proposes an enhanced semantic representation model for Airbus Service Bulletin (SB) texts by integrating an improved SimCSE framework with domain-specific lexicon injection. To address the challenge of accurately capturing highly technical and standardized terminology within SBs, we first construct a specialized aerospace maintenance dictionary. This lexicon is then utilized to generate domain-aware positive samples during the contrastive learning process of SimCSE, forcing the model to learn invariant representations for technical terms and their synonyms. The resulting model generates high-quality sentence embeddings that are both semantically rich and domain-sensitive. Evaluated on downstream tasks such as SB retrieval and classification, our approach demonstrates superior performance compared to baseline models, providing a robust foundation for automated processing and knowledge discovery in aerospace maintenance documentation.
authors: Z. Song; X. Guan; N. Zhao; J. Zhao
source: 2025 2nd International Conference on Machine Learning, Pattern Recognition and Automation Engineering (MLPRAE)
keywords: Contrastive Learning;Domain Adaptation;Service Bulletin (SB);Aerospace Maintenance;Natural Language Processing

title: CBR-Based Decision Support System for Maintenance Text Using NLP for an Aviation Case Study
abstract: Recently, Prognostics and Health Management (PHM) has emerged to promote predictive maintenance as a methodological key to overcome the limitations of traditional reliability analysis. The Natural Language Processing (NLP) methods allow the maintenance log usage for maintenance diagnostics and decision making. The Maintenance Work Orders (MWOs) contain vital health indicators and decades of experience related to various maintenance actions. However, due to the unstructured nature of maintenance text, it is not common to develop a tool using these textual maintenance entries. This paper proposes a textual Case-Based Reasoning (CBR) approach combined with Technical Language Processing (TLP) to find solutions for new problems based on previous experiences. The Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers (BERT) model is adopted for maintenance data using unsupervised finetuning technique Transformer-based Sequential Denoising AutoEncoder (TSDAE) for aviation case study. Results show that the pre-trained BERT model can adopt domain-specific data and produce semantic matches with only a small amount (1000 samples) of domain specific data.
authors: S. M. R. Naqvi; M. Ghufran; S. Meraghni; C. Varnier; J. -M. Nicod; N. Zerhouni
source: 2022 Prognostics and Health Management Conference (PHM-2022 London)
keywords: Prognostics and Health Management (PHM);Case-Based Reasoning;Natural Language Processing;Technical Language Processing;BERT;Sequential Denoising Auto-Encoder

title: Fast translation method of complex English sentences based on artificial intelligence technology
abstract: In order to optimize and improve the fast translation of complex English sentences, this paper proposes a fast translation method of complex English sentences based on artificial intelligence semantic text analysis model. A semantic structure parameter analysis model for rapid translation of English complex sentences is established, and quantitative feature analysis is carried out for rapid translation of English-Chinese complex sentences to obtain a quality analysis scale for rapid translation of English-Chinese complex sentences. The fuzzy decision-making method is adopted to comprehensively investigate the semantic features of sentences and the cognitive features of subjects, and a language cognitive perception model for rapid translation of complex English sentences is established. Through pattern analysis of language syntactic structure, semantic parameters are configured in the semantic text analysis model of artificial intelligence, which forms a text parameter analysis model for rapid translation of complex English sentences. The test results show that the scale analysis ability of this method for rapid translation of English-Chinese complex sentences is strong, the evaluation results are accurate and reliable, and the accuracy of rapid translation of English complex sentences is improved.
authors: G. Zhu
source: 2022 14th International Conference on Measuring Technology and Mechatronics Automation (ICMTMA)
keywords: Artificial intelligence technology;English;Complex statements;Quick translation

title: A Review on Leveraging Language Agnostic Automated Text Data Augmentation with Explainable Semantic Integrity
abstract: Data Augmentation (DA) is an effective strategy to increase model generalisation. In Natural Language Processing (NLP), DA remains in its early stages, primarily due to the inherent sensitivity of textual data, which complicates the augmentation process. These complexities are further increased in multilingual contexts, as DA techniques effective in one language may not yield comparable results in others. As a result, manually identifying optimal DA techniques that preserve semantic integrity and are language agnostic remains a significant challenge. It emphasises the necessity for automating the text DA process with Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI), which offers a promising approach by calculating word importance scores to effectively guide the augmentation. In this paper, a comprehensive review of existing approaches in auto-text DA is presented, beginning with an exploration of the concept of auto-text DA, followed by a discussion on its cross-lingual applications, and concluding with the integration of XAI for semantic preservation. The paper highlights the critical need for further research to enhance the effectiveness and applicability of XAI in auto-text DA, enabling its use across diverse languages.
authors: S. Wijesundera; S. M. Peiris
source: 2025 5th International Conference on Advanced Research in Computing (ICARC)
keywords: Automated Text Data Augmentation;Cross-Lingual Transfer Learning;Low-Resource Languages;Hyperparameter Optimisation;Explainable Artificial Intelligence

title: Intelligent Collection and Semantic Matching Algorithm for English-Chinese Corpus Based on Cluster Data Mining
abstract: This paper proposes the intelligent collection and algorithm matching of the English-Chinese material library based on database data mining. With the help of the part-of-speech dictionary in the “Concise Vocabulary Dictionary” published in “Modern Chinese” published in 2005, the conversion words in the English-Chinese dictionary are compared., Found that there are 30 conversion words, which are Chinese and English words. 666 species. This paper proposes an input feature recognition system that analyzes the input language of parallel corpus. To carry out different levels of corpus analysis, including grammar learning, shallow learning and demonstration of the Chinese language teaching system of students, design the corresponding English teaching system, compare the teaching effect in teaching, and study the teaching system.
authors: Y. Wei
source: 2022 Second International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Smart Energy (ICAIS)
keywords: Intelligent Collection;Semantic Matching;English-Chinese Corpus;Cluster Data Mining

title: Alignment-Supervised Bidimensional Attention-Based Recursive Autoencoders for Bilingual Phrase Representation
abstract: Exploiting semantic interactions between the source and target linguistic items at different levels of granularity is crucial for generating compact vector representations for bilingual phrases. To achieve this, we propose alignment-supervised bidimensional attention-based recursive autoencoders (ABattRAE) in this paper. ABattRAE first individually employs two recursive autoencoders to recover hierarchical tree structures of bilingual phrase, and treats the subphrase covered by each node on the tree as a linguistic item. Unlike previous methods, ABattRAE introduces a bidimensional attention network to measure the semantic matching degree between linguistic items of different languages, which enables our model to integrate information from all nodes by dynamically assigning varying weights to their corresponding embeddings. To ensure the accuracy of the generated attention weights in the attention network, ABattRAE incorporates word alignments as supervision signals to guide the learning procedure. Using the general stochastic gradient descent algorithm, we train our model in an end-to-end fashion, where the semantic similarity of translation equivalents is maximized while the semantic similarity of nontranslation pairs is minimized. Finally, we incorporate a semantic feature based on the learned bilingual phrase representations into a machine translation system for better translation selection. Experimental results on NIST Chinese-English and WMT English-German test sets show that our model achieves substantial improvements of up to 2.86 and 1.09 BLEU points over the baseline, respectively. Extensive in-depth analyses demonstrate the superiority of our model in learning bilingual phrase embeddings.
authors: B. Zhang; D. Xiong; J. Su; Y. Qin
source: IEEE Transactions on Cybernetics
keywords: Alignment supervision;attention network;bilingual phrase representation;machine translation;recursive autoencoder (RAE)

title: Siamese Networks for Multilingual Classified Ad Matching
abstract: This paper presents a novel approach to semantically match “Resource Wanted” and “Resource Offering” classified ads within Sri Lanka's complex multilingual digital marketplace. We introduce a Siamese neural network architecture specifically designed to effectively process both textual content and categorical metadata across English and Sinhala languages. Our model leverages advanced multilingual transformer models to create semantically rich embeddings, with a LaBSE-based implementation achieving superior performance, reaching a Recall@1 of 0.5813 and a Recall@10 of 0.9151. Crucially, the integration of categorical features with text embeddings yielded the best results, demonstrating a ${1. 5 \%}$ improvement in Recall@1 over the text-only approach. Our methodology addresses the significant challenge of matching ads across linguistic bound-aries in a low-resource setting, providing a method that can significantly improve transaction efficiency in Sri Lanka's diverse digital marketplace.
authors: P. Chathunka; D. Alwis; R. Rawishanka; I. Perera
source: 2025 Moratuwa Engineering Research Conference (MERCon)
keywords: multilingual classified ads;semantic matching;LaBSE;Siamese network;ad classification

title: LLM-Guided Crowdsourced Test Report Clustering
abstract: This paper proposes a clustering method for crowdsourced test reports based on a large language model to solve the limitations of existing methods in processing repeated reports and utilizing multi-modal information. Existing crowdsourced test report clustering methods have significant shortcomings in handling duplicate reports, ignoring the semantic information of screenshots, and underutilizing the relationship between text and images. The emergence of LLM provides a new way to solve these problems. By integrating the semantic understanding ability of LLM, key information can be extracted from the test report more accurately, and the semantic relationship between screenshots and text descriptions can be used to guide the clustering process, thus improving the accuracy and effectiveness of clustering. The method in this paper uses a pre-trained LLM (such as GPT-4) to encode the text in the test report, and uses a visual model such as CLIP to encode the application screenshots, converting the text descriptions and images into high-dimensional semantic vectors. The cosine similarity is then used to calculate the similarity between the vectors, and semantic binding rules are constructed to guide the clustering process, ensuring that semantically related reports are assigned to the same cluster and semantically different reports are assigned to different clusters. Through experimental verification, this method is significantly superior to traditional methods in several evaluation indicators, demonstrating its great potential in improving the efficiency and quality of crowdsourced test report processing. In the future, this method is expected to be widely used in the process of software testing and maintenance, and further promote technological progress.
authors: Y. Li; Y. Zhong; L. Yang; Y. Wang; P. Zhu
source: IEEE Access
keywords: Large language model;crowdsourced testing;test report clustering

title: Enhancing Code Security Specification Detection in Software Development with LLM
abstract: In order to improve the accuracy and efficiency of code security specification detection in the software development process, a large-scale language model (LLM)-based detection architecture is constructed to realize deep semantic parsing and structured rule matching for high-complexity code in multilingual environments. The research designs a security specification controlled language system integrating CFG and AST, covering 62 rule items, 480 types of semantic labels and 3000 AST nodes, and introduces a quintuple language model G = (N, Σ, P, S, C) to construct five types of key constraint domains, including variable naming, privilege boundaries, input validation and so on. The structural semantic graph with 2.1 × 106 edges is constructed by graph neural network, and a deep detection model containing 690 million parameters is designed to support sliding window scanning and semantic embedding matching, adapting to 45 kinds of security constraint structures. The experiments are tested in 1.38 million function fragments, and the detection accuracy is more than 93%, among which the accuracy of memory access out-of-bounds class reaches 95.6%, which verifies the high adaptability and stability of the model in the aspects of function privilege control, input validation and memory security.
authors: S. Zhan; Y. Lin; Y. Yao; J. Zhu
source: 2025 7th International Conference on Information Science, Electrical and Automation Engineering (ISEAE)
keywords: code security;specification detection;LLM

title: Sankalp: AI-Powered Career Guidance System
abstract: Traditional career guidance systems often lack personalization, adaptability, and accessibility, leading to suboptimal decisions in a rapidly changing job market. This study introduces Sankalp, an AI-driven career guidance framework that combines semantic reasoning, emotion analysis, and adaptive learning to provide inclusive and explainable recommendations. Built on a multi-agent hybrid architecture, Sankalp integrates modules for semantic matching using Sentence-BERT (SBERT), emotion-aware feedback through Valence Aware Dictionary and Sentiment Reasoner (VADER) sentiment analysis, a Naukri API–based job trends agent, a knowledge graph, and a voice assistant that supports English, Hindi, and Kannada. A reinforcement learning–based fusion layer dynamically refines recommendations based on real-time user feedback, modeling career choice as a life-span adaptive process. It was evaluated against a Curated Ground Truth (CGT) dataset using 160 participant profiles, Sankalp demonstrated superior performance, achieving a Top-1 Hit Rate of 77.5%, a Top-3 Hit Rate of 90.5%, and a high mean user satisfaction score of 4.71/5.0. The latency analysis confirmed high operational efficiency, with an average end-to-end response time of 1.25 s. Through its inclusive design and adaptive, explainable hybrid AI, Sankalp enhances the accessibility, reliability, and ethical delivery of career guidance services, aligning with the global sustainable development goals.
authors: S. Nandi; M. M; B. Aishwarya Nayak; B. Sathish Babu
source: IEEE Access
keywords: AI-driven career guidance;emotion analysis;google cloud speech-to-text;knowledge graph;multi-agent architecture;multilingual support;natural language processing;personalized recommendations;rule-based AI;Sankalp;sentence-BERT;sustainable development goals;VADER sentiment analysis;voice assistant

title: Computational Approaches to Cross-Cultural Multimodal Film and TV Integration Using AMTAs
abstract: How to efficiently translate across modalities is an important challenge in the field of machine learning. Therefore, this study proposes an innovative algorithm based on the hybrid variational multi-modal framework (AMTAs) for the problem of automatic machine translation in cross-cultural multi-modal integration of film and television data. The core innovation of this study lies in the construction of a hybrid variational multi-modal translation framework. The algorithm adopts a three-level processing architecture: (1) The multimodal semantic matching layer realizes cross-modal feature alignment through the difference map generator. At the same time, the improved SimCSE model is used to calculate the cosine similarity. This can dynamically extract the semantic difference word set; (2) The hybrid variational inference layer introduces the K -component Gaussian mixture model. This model dynamically adjusts the contribution of each component through the conditional weight network. The weight calculation uses the Softmax normalized feed-forward neural network output; (3) The small sample optimization layer designs a negative sampling strategy based on contrastive learning. This strategy sets the InfoNCE loss function with a temperature coefficient 0.05. The experiment used 150,000 sentences of bilingual film and television corpus for verification. The results showed that in the metaphor recognition task, the algorithm's F1 value reached 77.2%, an increase of 13.1 % over the baseline model; the BLEU-4 score in the multi-modal translation task increased by 9.8 percentage points. These empirical results verify the effectiveness of the proposed algorithm.
authors: H. Zhong
source: 2025 6th International Conference on Data Intelligence and Cognitive Informatics (ICDICI)
keywords: Automatic Machine Translation Algorithms (AMTAs);Cross-cultural Communication;Multi-modal Data Integration;Film and Television Media;Semantic Alignment

title: Automated System for Monitoring and Evaluation of Environmental Document Revision Based on Text Extraction and Data Analysis Technology
abstract: This research aims to design an automated system for monitoring and evaluating environmental document revisions, particularly in the context of Environmental Impact Analysis (EIA) and Environmental Management and Monitoring Efforts (UKL-UPL). This system leverages technologies such as natural language processing (NLP), optical character recognition (OCR), and document management to enhance the efficiency and accuracy of the document revision process. The system is divided into several stages: document uploading, text extraction from various formats using tools such as Apache Tika and PyMuPDF, feedback analysis through integration with Google Sheets, and text matching with semantic analysis using SBERT embeddings. The final evaluation is performed automatically and generates a report containing suggestions and feedback that have been completed. In the experimental results, five of eleven suggestions from the revised UKL-UPL documents were successfully detected as “Completed” with a similarity threshold of $\geqslant 0.75$. The remaining six suggestions showed lower similarity scores and were marked as “Not Completed.” This indicates that the system successfully identified some of the addressed suggestions, with potential for improvement in detection accuracy. Automating document revisions' evaluation ensures a more transparent, consistent, and reliable process, reducing manual errors and delays. This approach aligns with the growing need for digital solutions in environmental document management, enhancing operational efficiency and compliance with environmental regulations.
authors: A. Prasetyo; S. H. Supangkat; D. W. Anggara
source: 2025 International Conference on ICT for Smart Society (ICISS)
keywords: Environmental documents;Automated system;Text extraction;NLP;Document revision

title: KnowLog: Knowledge Enhanced Pretrained Language Model for Log Understanding
abstract: Logs as semi-structured text are rich in semantic information, making their comprehensive understanding crucial for automated log analysis. With the recent success of pretrained language models in natural language processing, many studies have leveraged these models to understand logs. Despite their successes, existing pretrained language models still suffer from three weaknesses. Firstly, these models fail to understand domain-specific terminol-ogy, especially abbreviations. Secondly, these models struggle to adequately capture the complete log context information. Thirdly, these models have difficulty in obtaining universal representations of different styles of the same logs. To address these challenges, we introduce KnowLog, a knowledge-enhanced pretrained language model for log understanding. Specifically, to solve the previous two challenges, we exploit abbreviations and natural language de-scriptions of logs from public documentation as local and global knowledge, respectively, and leverage this knowledge by designing novel pretraining tasks for enhancing the model. To solve the last challenge, we design a contrastive learning-based pretraining task to obtain universal representations. We evaluate KnowLog by fine-tuning it on six different log understanding tasks. Extensive experiments demonstrate that KnowLog significantly enhances log understanding and achieves state-of-the-art results compared to existing pretrained language models without knowledge enhancement. Moreover, we conduct additional experiments in transfer learning and low-resource scenarios, showcasing the substantial advantages of KnowLog. Our source code and detailed experimental data are available at https://github.com/LeaperOvO/KnowLog.
authors: L. Ma; W. Yang; B. Xu; S. Jiang; B. Fei; J. Liang; M. Zhou; Y. Xiao
source: 2024 IEEE/ACM 46th International Conference on Software Engineering (ICSE)
keywords: pretrained language model;knowledge enhancement;log understanding

title: IndoColSmol: Lightweight Vision-Language Models for Multimodal Indonesian Document Search
abstract: Recent progress in document retrieval increasingly leverages vision-language models that bypass OCR by jointly encoding both visual and textual content. These models capture layout, font, and image features typically ignored by text-only approaches. However, state-of-the-art architectures often require substantial computational resources, limiting their applicability in real-time or low-resource environments. Lightweight alternatives such as ColFlor and ColSmolVLM improve efficiency but are primarily trained on English corpora, resulting in reduced effectiveness in Indonesian settings. This work introduces IndoColSmol-256M and IndoColSmol-500M, two compact vision-language retrievers optimized for Indonesian document image collections. Based on the ColSmol architecture, both models apply a ColBERT-style late interaction mechanism, where each query and document is represented by multiple embeddings, and relevance is computed through fine-grained pairwise similarity between all query and document vectors. This strategy balances retrieval accuracy and computational efficiency. We perform contrastive fine-tuning on Indonesian image-query pairs, allowing the models to learn semantic alignment without relying on OCR or token-level supervision. Evaluation on the Indonesian Multimodal Open Question Answering (IMOQA) benchmark shows that IndoColSmol-256M and IndoColSmol-500M achieve Recall@5 scores of 0.88764 and 0.89045 respectively, outperforming multimodal baselines such as CLIP and SIGLIP. Both models offer low memory usage and inference latency, making them suitable for deployment in constrained environments such as mobile search, internal document lookup systems, and offline access tools. This study recommends further research on region-level alignment and multilingual training strategies to extend compact vision-language retrieval to additional low-resource languages and specialized document domains.
authors: J. M. Fritz; N. I. Utama; R. Y. Fa'Rifah
source: 2025 International Conference on Data Science and Its Applications (ICoDSA)
keywords: multimodal document embedding;information retrieval;Indonesian NLP;lightweight models;OCR-free retrieval

title: Government Schemes Recommendation API with Multi-Language Chatbot using RAG Vector Search and Conversational AI
abstract: Complicated eligibility rules, disjointed information as well as language barriers makes it difficult in most cases to navigate government welfare schemes with its citizens. Traditional online websites and applications often do not offer customized instructions, language inclusion, and easy interaction floors, which leads to a low level of usage of the necessary public services. To overcome these drawbacks, an intelligent recommendation system has been created to utilize Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), semantic vector similarity search and multilingual conversational artificial intelligence. The system derives semantic embeddings via Facebook AI Similarity Search (FAISS) to facilitate effective information retrieval and comprises Google Generative AI to take advantage of natural language understanding. LangChain is used to support dynamic, contextually aware conversation, and FastAPI is introduced as the framework to permit an easy flow of integration. It was developed in multiple languages with real time translation between English and Tamil to provide ease of use by different groups of users. The system provides a single API that can recognize the intent of the user, check his/her eligibility and suggest the appropriate schemes in a language that adjusts to the user. The method fosters digital inclusions, simplifying delivery of public services and facilitating access to government welfare programs on equal basis.
authors: V. S. Reddy; J. B. B. Bell
source: 2025 4th International Conference on Innovative Mechanisms for Industry Applications (ICIMIA)
keywords: Natural Language Processing;Government Schemes;Conversational AI;FAISS;Retrieval-Augmented Generation;Multi-language Support;FastAPI

title: Personalized Recommendation Algorithm Based on Graph Embedding and Temporal Context Representation Learning
abstract: With the rapid development of deep learning technology and the wide application of intelligent recommendation systems, how to apply deep learning technology to intelligent recommendation systems to continuously improve the recommendation effect has always been a hot research direction. User historical behavior information plays an increasingly important role in the evolution and iteration of the recommender system model. Focusing on how to make full use of the time context of user behavior, how to better deal with the sparsity of massive data and system cold-start problem, the personalized recommendation algorithm is proposed. This algorithm represents the time sequence behavior data with the graph network structure, uses the graph representation learning model node2vec to obtain the graph embedding representation of users and items in the hyperparameter space, and introduces IF_ISF for the negative sampling to improve the model efficiency, and then generates the embedding representation of the user temporal context by the sum-pooling method, and also generates the embedding representation of the static attribute information of users and items by the deep semantic matching method based on DSSM, and finally concatenates the feature vector and uses the machine learning model XGBoost for recommendation ranking prediction. The ablation experiments through the comparison of multiple models on the MovieLens_1M public dataset show that the proposed algorithm is better than the baseline algorithm on the three indicators of MAE, Macro_F1 and Micro_F1, which has different degrees of improvement and can effectively improve the recommendation accuracy and satisfaction.
authors: J. Yu; Y. Li; J. Shi; W. Liu; X. Liu; M. Zhou; Y. Chen; N. Sai
source: 2025 IEEE 17th International Conference on Computer Research and Development (ICCRD)
keywords: graph embedding;temporal context;representation learning;personalized recommendation;rank learning

title: Research on Correcting the Accuracy of English Translation Based on Improved Particle Swarm Optimization Algorithm
abstract: With the development of cognitive technology, replacing manual English translation(ET) with machines has gradually come into people's sight, and the accuracy of translation is also constantly improving. When computers and other machines are used to translate English content, it often happens that a word is translated incorrectly or the context is not smooth due to the improper recognition of the word content and context, which is one of the shortcomings of traditional machine ET. In order to improve the intelligence level of machine ET, this paper proposes a method of correcting the accuracy of machine ET based on particle swarm optimization (PSO), constructs an information extraction model of machine ET, selects the optimal solution of fuzzy semantics, and draws a valid conclusion. The results show that the accuracy and correction ability of the model can significantly eliminate errors in ET, and can effectively improve the accuracy and rationality of ET.
authors: L. Zhao; Y. Zheng
source: 2023 IEEE International Conference on Sensors, Electronics and Computer Engineering (ICSECE)
keywords: ET;Particle swarm optimization;Intelligent;Accuracy

title: The Importance of Transformational Method in Automatic Idiom Translation
abstract: This study aims to study the effectiveness of transformational methods in the process of automatic translation of idioms from the Uzbek language into English. The polysemy, cultural loading and context-dependent nature of phrases create significant problems in their machine translation. The study analyzed 3547 phraseological units taken from the Explanatory Dictionary of the Uzbek language and the uzschoolcorpora.uz national corpus, and their semantic equivalents in English were determined based on the British National Corpus (BNC) and the Oxford Dictionary of Idioms. The transformational model was developed based on syntactic structure, semantic similarity and statistical correspondence. The results show that the transformational approach has a significant advantage over literal translation in terms of BLEU (Bilingual Evaluation Understudy) and METEOR (Metric for Evaluation of Translation with Explicit Ordering) indicators, ensuring contextually and culturally accurate translation of idioms. This model can be implemented as an effective tool for improving the quality of automatic translation and enriching scientific approaches in the field of computational linguistics and translation studies.
authors: M. Abjalova; U. Rashidova; S. Sharipova
source: 2025 10th International Conference on Computer Science and Engineering (UBMK)
keywords: transformational method;idioms;machine translation;database of Uzbek idioms;transformational translation model;BNC

title: Speech-Content-Driven Highlighting of Translated Lecture Slides for Foreign Language Lecture Understanding
abstract: This paper presents a lecture support system to assist international students in understanding lectures delivered in a non-native language. The system integrates speech recognition, character recognition (OCR), machine translation, and content-based highlighting. It transcribes speech using “Whisper,” translates it with “DeepL,” and extracts slide text and OCR using “YomiToku.” “Sentence Transformers” computes similarities between spoken content and slide text, highlighting relevant translated sections. An evaluation involving 19 international students compared three system configurations: highlighting only, slide translation only, and the proposed integrated system. The evaluation results showed that the integrated approach significantly outperformed both single-feature configurations, with statistically significant differences in pairwise comparisons ($\mathbf{p}<\mathbf{0. 0 5}$) and score improvements of $\mathbf{2 0 - 4 6}$ points. The integrated system particularly benefited students with lower Japanese proficiency, demonstrating substantial improvements in their ability to follow the relationship between spoken content and slide information.
authors: N. Muto; C. S. Leow; J. Hoshino; T. Utsuro; H. Nishizaki
source: 2025 Asia Pacific Signal and Information Processing Association Annual Summit and Conference (APSIPA ASC)

title: AI-Powered Text-to-SQL Translation: Enabling Natural Language Interfaces for Databases
abstract: The complexity of Structured Query Language (SQL) creates a barrier for non-technical users who need to access insights from relational databases. This paper presents an AI-powered framework for translating natural language queries into SQL, aiming to bridge the gap between user intent and structured data access. By leveraging advancements in Natural Language Processing (NLP), particularly transformer-based models like T5 and BART, we propose a hybrid neural-symbolic architecture that incorporates schema-aware embeddings, SQL grammar constraints, and contextual understanding of user queries. Our system is designed to generalize across domains and handle complex query structures, including nested and multi-table queries. We evaluate its performance using benchmark datasets such as Spider and demonstrate real-world utility through integration with a business intelligence chatbot for retail sales analytics. The model achieves high execution accuracy and semantic match rates, outperforming baseline models like Seq2SQL and SQLNet. We also present a detailed error analysis and discuss limitations in handling ambiguous or underspecified queries. This work contributes a practical solution for democratizing data access in enterprise environments and sets the stage for further enhancements such as multilingual support, interactive query clarification, and integration with visualization platforms. Our approach empowers users of all technical backgrounds to extract meaningful insights from data through natural language interaction.
authors: P. Nutalapati; R. Keshava; N. Seshagiri; B. M. Harve; D. Kole; I. Sahoo; V. Soni
source: 2025 5th International Conference on Intelligent Cybernetics Technology & Applications (ICICyTA)
keywords: Natural Language Processing (NLP);Transformer Models;Semantic Parsing;Structured Query Language (SQL);Schema Linking

title: Swings and Roundabouts: Attention-Structure Interaction Effect in Deep Semantic Matching
abstract: In the context of deep learning models for semantic matching problems, we propose a novel Multi-View Progressive Attention (MV-PA) mechanism general enough to operate on various linguistic structures of text. More importantly, we study the interaction effect between explicit linguistic structures (e.g., linear, constituency, and dependency) and implicit structures elicited by attention mechanisms. Empirical results on multiple datasets demonstrate salient patterns of substitutability between the two families of structures (explicit and implicit). Our findings not only provide intellectual foundations for the popular use of “linear LSTM + attention” architectures in NLP/QA research, but also have implications in other modalities and domains.
authors: A. Gupta; Z. Zhang
source: IEEE/ACM Transactions on Audio, Speech, and Language Processing
keywords: Semantic matching;attention mechanisms;long short-term memory networks;tree-LSTM

title: Research on Comparative Modeling of Free Trade Agreement Clauses With Deep Semantic Integration
abstract: In the evolving landscape of computational sciences and interdisciplinary research, accurately comparing clauses within free trade agreements (FTAs) poses a formidable challenge. The legal language inherent to these documents is highly nuanced, context-dependent, and semantically intricate. Traditional clause comparison techniques-such as keyword matching, lexicon-based metrics, or syntactic rules-often fall short in capturing deep contextual relationships, overlook implicit legal meanings, and struggle to handle terminological variations across agreements. These limitations significantly impair the reliability of comparative legal analysis, hinder informed policymaking, and obstruct effective compliance monitoring. To address these challenges, a novel deep semantic model is proposed, integrating clause-level graphs with transformer-driven encoders in a unified architecture. The system leverages pre-trained language models to generate rich semantic embeddings of legal clauses and utilizes Multilateral Normative Integration Model (MNIM) to model inter-clause dependencies and structural relationships. This hybrid architecture enables our method to detect both overt and subtle semantic alignments, identify conceptual shifts, and measure clause similarity with high contextual awareness. Empirical evaluations conducted on a diverse and multilingual corpus of international FTAs demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach. The proposed method achieves up to 15% higher alignment accuracy and a 20% improvement in F1 score compared to traditional and state-of-the-art baselines. These advancements support scalable, high-precision alignment of legal texts, enabling practical applications in automated policy drafting, legal analytics, and international regulatory harmonization. By bridging the gap between domain-specific legal interpretation and cutting-edge semantic modeling, our framework contributes a powerful, interpretable tool that advances both computational legal studies and broader interdisciplinary innovation.
authors: G. Zhang
source: IEEE Access
keywords: Deep semantic modeling;transformer-based encoders;clause alignment;legal text analysis;interdisciplinary computational sciences

title: Fast Retrieval Algorithm of Multilingual Translation Corpus Based on Distributed Storage
abstract: With the acceleration of globalization, massive multilingual translation corpora in distributed storage environments present core problems such as strong data heterogeneity and low cross-node retrieval efficiency. To this end, this study proposes a cross-node retrieval optimization framework based on a hybrid index structure. Firstly, this paper designs a multi-dimensional feature coder for semantic units. Then, this paper constructs a distributed hierarchical index structure based on improved LSH (Locality-Sensitive Hashing). Finally, a dynamic load balancing algorithm based on spark is developed. Experimental results show that on the Europarl and UN Parallel 2.1 TB multilingual corpus, the Top-10 retrieval accuracy is as high as 91%, and the Hybrid scheduling strategy achieves 800 QPS throughput under 1500 concurrent conditions. In the above data conclusions, this framework performs well in retrieval accuracy, real-time responsiveness and high concurrent throughput, providing scalable technical support for real-time retrieval of cross-border language service systems.
authors: B. Xu
source: 2025 3rd International Conference on Data Science and Network Security (ICDSNS)
keywords: distributed storage;multilingual corpus retrieval;semantic feature fusion;locality sensitive hashing;retrieval algorithm

title: Zero-Shot Prompting for LLM-Based Machine Translation Using In-Domain Target Sentences
abstract: One promising aspect of using large language models (LLMs) for translation is their ability to adapt effectively to unseen domains without fine-tuning, through strategic prompting. However, the success of this approach largely depends on the selection of relevant examples from domain-specific databases to construct the few-shot prompt for the given input. This work propose a novel zero-shot technique that circumvents data limitations by generating synthetic translation examples from monolingual domain-specific target texts. Our preliminary experiments suggest that LLMs demonstrate increased sensitivity to noise on the target side of examples, while maintaining a tolerance for variations in example quality. Based on these findings, we generate pseudo-examples by using a cross-lingual sentence encoder to identify sentences in the target language that correspond to the input, and then pairing them with coarse translations derived from a dictionary. To improve the retrieval of better pseudo-examples for translation tasks, we enhance the sentence encoder by incorporating domain information through conditional layer normalization and introducing two innovative training objectives: LLM Preference Prediction and Windowed Sentence Prediction. Experiments conducted across various domains demonstrate that our approach not only significantly outperforms previous zero-shot methods but is also comparable to the robust NLLB-3.3B and ALMA-13B(-R) model.
authors: B. Ji; X. Duan; Y. Zhang; K. Wu; M. Zhang
source: IEEE Transactions on Audio, Speech and Language Processing
keywords: Domain adaptation;LLM-based machine translation;zero-shot

title: Research on the Application of Knowledge Graph Reconstruction and Traceability Method Based on Event Chain in Power Data Security Operation and Maintenance
abstract: With the improvement of the reliability requirements of power production and the strict requirements of state Grid Corporation on the power supply service quality, the traditional power operation and maintenance mode has been difficult to meet the current demand. Therefore, this paper proposes to use knowledge graph technology to integrate, analyze and visualized power data to improve the efficiency and accuracy of power operation and maintenance. By constructing the multi-source heterogeneous information model and the deep semantic matching model of the electric power system, the fusion of heterogeneous data and feature extraction are realized. The power data knowledge graph is built, taking the equipment fault alarm event as an example, and the application effect of the power data knowledge graph in real-time monitoring and early warning, fault location and maintenance decision is shown.
authors: Z. Zou; B. Ye; S. Fan; X. Zhang
source: 2025 3rd International Conference On Mobile Internet, Cloud Computing and Information Security (MICCIS)
keywords: power data;knowledge graph;event chain;reconstruction and traceability;security operation and maintenance

title: P-BERT: Toward Long Sequence Modeling by Enabling Language Representation With Prefix Sequence Compression, Soft Position Embedding, and Data Augmentation for Patent Relevance Assessment
abstract: Recent works have increasingly adopted pre-trained language models, such as BERT, to model technical semantics for patent relevance assessment. However, existing truncation and divide-merge strategies, used to handle input length constraints, results in feature loss and semantic isolation. This limits the effectiveness of pre-trained language models in capturing long sequence semantics for patent relevance assessment. Because patent relevance is evaluated based on local sequence semantics, this paper proposes a patent-oriented bidirectional encoder representation from transformers (P-BERT) to enable the semantic modeling ability of long sequences. The characteristics of P-BERT lie in a prefix sequence compression method, a soft position embedding method and a data augmentation method. The first one can retrain more technical features within the input constraints via transforming full sequences into partial sequences while the second one reconstructs local sequence semantics for partial sequences. As for the last one, it contains sample and feature augmentation to improve the generalization and robustness. Due to the scarcity of training corpus for patent relevance assessment, a dual constraint strategy is proposed to generate a supervised corpus, DualCons. Experimental results demonstrate that DualCons can help BERT obtain the best ranking performance compared with other strategies. Model evaluations show the effectiveness of P-BERT. It reaches an improvement of 2%-6% in recall-oriented metrics and an improvement of 1%-5% in accuracy-oriented indicators over the state-of-the-art baselines. All those would promote the engineering applications of pre-trained language models in patent relevance assessment.
authors: F. Wang; X. Shi; D. Wang; Y. Lou
source: IEEE Access
keywords: Prefix sequence compression;soft position embedding;data augmentation;patent relevance assessment

title: Semantic Relationship Model of Chinese International Core Vocabulary in Artificial Intelligence Scenarios
abstract: The rapid progress of Artificial Intelligence (AI) has gained widespread attention worldwide, and its applications in industries have inspired a strong interest in the semantic relationship. As one of the hotspots of AI research, Chinese international core words have been valued and favored by scholars. An in-depth exploration was conducted in this study for the semantic connections of Chinese international core words in AI with a literature review. There were disagreements on the definition of word concepts in AI, which directly affects theoretical innovation and application. With the continuous development of science and technology, numerous vocabularies are added, and their meanings are constantly changing. At present, the academic community still lacks an in-depth understanding of the relationship between the Chinese International Core Vocabulary and AI so it is necessary to research it. Combining the historical evolution of terms, the semantic meanings at different times and contexts were investigated to provide a basis for further study. Existing studies were limited at the lexical level and lacked in-depth exploration from the syntactic perspective, thus failing to demonstrate the complex semantic relationships among Chinese international core vocabulary. In this study, the Semantic Relational Model of International Chinese Core Vocabulary in AI Scenarios (SRMCCV) was developed based on linguistic theory and computer processing technology. Statistical analysis methods such as word frequency statistics were used to explore the semantic logic of frequently used words and analyze relevant literature and new corpus. The model revealed the potential connections between words and semantic interactions in AI. The results can be used to improve the quality of international Chinese language teaching.
authors: Y. Zhu
source: 2023 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Social Sciences and Intelligence Management (SSIM)
keywords: AI;international Chinese language;core vocabulary;semantic relations

title: Knowledge Representation Learning with Hyperboloid Models
abstract: Knowledge graph describes the objective knowledge in the real world in a structured form. Knowledge representation learning aims to project the entities and relations in the knowledge graph into a low-dimensional, continuous vector space and learn vector representations of the semantic information of entities and relations. Moreover, link prediction and other tasks are carried out by vector calculation, and a variety of downstream tasks are achieved while retaining the structure of the knowledge graph itself. However, the existing knowledge representation learning models have certain defects when modeling complex relationships such as 1-N, N-1, and N-N, and the processing effect is not well. In order to better deal with complex relations, this paper proposes a knowledge representation learning model based on hyperboloid model, which emitters the knowledge graph into hyperbolic space for knowledge representation learning. Experiments are carried out on the public datasets FB15K, FB15K-237 and WN18RR. The experimental results show that the proposed model is superior to the baseline model in most evaluation indicators, which proves effectiveness of the proposed model.
authors: Y. Li; J. Gan; J. Wang; X. Han; M. Wang
source: 2023 16th International Conference on Advanced Computer Theory and Engineering (ICACTE)
keywords: Knowledge graph;Knowledge representation learning;Hyperbolic space;Hyperboloid model

title: Exploring Topic Supervision with BERT for Text Matching
abstract: Text matching is a critical task in natural language processing to measure semantic similarity between two texts. A significant portion of online texts are labeled with a variety of coarse topic responses. These supervised topic indicators can provide prior structured and explicable semantics for textual similarity modeling. However, most existing state-of-the-art neural network methods cannot benefit from such complementary topic signals. Therefore, we propose a novel Topic Supervision BERT-based model (TSB) for text matching. TSB provides a reference multi-task joint training framework involving two types of topic supervision, including explicit and implicit topic supervision. To constrain consistent topic correspondences between texts, we introduce a supervised auxiliary learning task to incorporate explicit pre-defined topic supervision. Furthermore, to adapt to latent topic structures for mutual benefit between text representations and multiple tasks, we integrate a topic model into a contextual text representation model BERT to mine and incorporate implicit self-learnable topic supervision. Experimental results show that TSB supplements explicit and implicit topic information through a multi-task learning approach, which significantly improves the performance of text matching on two public datasets, especially on challenging short text matches.
authors: Y. Wang; M. Xu; Y. Yan; T. Zhao; Y. Chen; J. Yang
source: 2022 International Joint Conference on Neural Networks (IJCNN)
keywords: Text Matching;Topic Supervision;Semantic Similarity;Multi-Task Learning;Pre-trained Model

title: Interactive Attention Networks for Semantic Text Matching
abstract: Semantic text matching, which matches target texts to source texts, is a general problem in many areas, such as information retrieval, question answering, and recommendation. The challenges to existing research on this topic include 1) out-of-vocabulary and low-frequency keywords and 2) direct utilization of sparse matching matrix of source and target. The out-of-vocabulary and low-frequency keywords could lead to the mismatch of similar keywords in source and target texts. The sparse matching matrix cannot provide enough clues to match the source with the target. To address these challenges, we propose a novel deep neural semantic text matching model. Our model adopts an interactive attention network to achieve information exchange between the source text and the target text, and dynamically explores the matching matrix and learns new representations of source and target texts. Experimental results on three different text matching datasets demonstrate that our model can significantly outperform competitive baselines. Furthermore, our model demonstrates great advantage in alleviating the sparse matching problem and learning out-of-vocabulary words with the local context, which widely exists in a broad spectrum of NLP applications.
authors: S. Zhao; Y. Huang; C. Su; Y. Li; F. Wang
source: 2020 IEEE International Conference on Data Mining (ICDM)
keywords: text semantic matching;deep neural networks;interactive attention;sparse matching;out-of-vocabulary words;information retrieval;question answering;tweet linking

title: Legal Feature Enhanced Semantic Matching Network for Similar Case Matching
abstract: Similar case matching (SCM) aims to determine whether legal case documents are similar or not. In fact, SCM is an extension of the semantic text matching. Various deep learning models are proposed to solve the semantic text matching problems. However, the main difference between the case documents may be subtle, and the length of documents can be quite long. Moreover, the case documents are written in structural format and contain plenty of legal terms. To address these challenges, we propose a novel model in this paper. Accordingly, the legal feature vector is introduced into the semantic text matching model, and BERT is adopted as the encoding layer to capture long-range dependencies in the case documents. We conduct several experiments to evaluate the performance of our proposed model. The results show that our model outperforms other existing methods on the public dataset CAIL2019-SCM.
authors: Z. Hong; Q. Zhou; R. Zhang; W. Li; T. Mo
source: 2020 International Joint Conference on Neural Networks (IJCNN)
keywords: Similar case matching;BERT;Attention mechanism;Law intelligence

title: Design of Semantic Web Distributed Retrieval System Based on Rule Reasoning Algorithm
abstract: Ontology technology is the core technology of semantic web, which can describe information well and retrieve described information through querying knowledge warehouses, greatly improving retrieval efficiency and accuracy. Distributed retrieval is the process of combining heterogeneous data stored on a large scale through a network, utilizing the processing capabilities of multiple machines to centrally solve collaborative indexing and retrieval problems, and ultimately forming a logical whole. Firstly, the semantic search architecture was studied; Then, the AMIE+rule inference algorithm was studied, including rules and functions, the process of AMIE+mining algorithm, and evaluation criteria; Next, the process of rule-based reasoning algorithm was studied; Finally, experimental analysis was conducted, and four algorithms including Tableau algorithm, Apriori algorithm, Rete algorithm, and AMIE+algorithm were selected for comparison, demonstrating the superiority of AMIE+algorithm and providing a basis for subsequent software system development.
authors: L. Zhao; Y. Liu
source: 2024 4th Asia-Pacific Conference on Communications Technology and Computer Science (ACCTCS)
keywords: rule reasoning;semantic Web;distributed retrieval;system design;AMIE+ Mining algorithm

title: Knowledge Link Inference of Graph Structure Based on Holographic Model
abstract: For current knowledge link inference methods, whether it is traditional translation models, semantic matching models, or convolutional neural network models, it is impossible to obtain rich semantic information. This paper mainly uses a pre-training layer based on the holographic model, and combines the knowledge structure to perform the knowledge link inference. Firstly, the pre-training layer is used as the model initialization. Secondly, the graph structure encoder layer not only combines the information of entities and relationships directly related to the current entity, but also considers the information including multi-hop neighbor nodes and auxiliary relationships. Finally, ConvKB is used as a decoder to score the triples. The model is evaluated on two benchmark datasets WN18RR and FB237, that is slightly better than the previous embedding models on some indicators.
authors: Y. Zhao; L. Chen; G. Zeng; C. Zhang
source: 2020 12th International Conference on Advanced Computational Intelligence (ICACI)
keywords: holographic model;graph structure;link inference

title: A Fedarated Pseudonymization Framework for Privacy-Preservating LLM's
abstract: As the adoption of cloud-based Large Language Models (LLMs) expands, safeguarding Personally Identifiable Information (PII) has become a critical challenge-particularly in sensitive domains like healthcare, finance, and legal services. This work proposes a novel Federated Contextual Pseudonymization Framework with Adaptive Fine-Tuning, designed to enable secure, real-time interaction with LLMs without compromising data utility. Leveraging advanced Named Entity Recognition (NER) techniques and federated learning, the framework ensures that sensitive data is pseudonymized at the source and never centralized during training. Tools such as Hugging Face Transformers, spaCy, and POS tagging modules are integrated to enable precise detection of both common and context-specific entities across distributed nodes. The system supports adaptive fine-tuning, enhancing performance across diverse linguistic styles and domains. Crucially, a secure reidentification mechanism maps anonymized tokens back to original values only when authorized, preserving personalization in outputs. Empirical results demonstrate strong performance: 97.2% precision in PII detection, a 95.8% F1-score in multilingual settings, and a 28 % reduction in latency. This architecture balances privacy, compliance (GDPR, HIPAA), and scalability— making it suitable for high-stakes applications such as diagnostic assistants, legal automation, and secure customer service bots.
authors: S. C; K. S. R; K. S; J. P. V; A. J
source: 2025 10th International Conference on Communication and Electronics Systems (ICCES)
keywords: Federated Learning;Pseudonymization;Privacy-Preserving AI;Adaptive Fine-Tuning;Named Entity Recognition;Personally Identifiable Information

title: Optimizing Context-Enhanced Relational Joins
